1.title: The God of heaven
1.text: Cyrus declares that 'the Lord God of heaven' gave him all the kingdoms of the earth and charged him to build a house in Jerusalem.

1.question: How did the neighbors help those going up to Jerusalem?
options: With silver, gold, goods, beasts and precious things

1.question: Who brought the gold and silver vessels from Babylon up to Jerusalem?

2.title: The return with Zerubbabel
2.text: The list names Zerubbabel and Jeshua among the leaders who led the exiles back from Babylon to Jerusalem and Judah.

2.question: How many were the children of Arah?
How many were the children of Bani?
How many were the children of Adin?
How many were the children of Gibbar?
How many were the children of Kirjath-arim, Chephirah and Beeroth?
How many were the children of Magbish?
How many were the children of Senaah?
How many were the Levites, the children of Jeshua and Kadmiel?
Whose children are listed among the Nethinims in verse 45?
Which groups are mentioned among the Nethinims in verse 50?
Whose children close the list of the Nethinims?
How many were the children of Delaiah, Tobiah and Nekoda?
How many menservants and maidservants were there, besides the congregation?
Where did the priests, Levites and people dwell on returning?

3.title: The altar before the temple
3.text: Jeshua and Zerubbabel first build the altar of the God of Israel to offer burnt offerings, as written in the law of Moses, even before the temple foundation is laid.

3.question: Which offering did the Israelites resume besides the set feasts?
Who stood up 'as one' to direct the workmen of the house of God?
options: Jeshua, his sons and brethren, with Kadmiel

3.question: Why could the people not tell the sounds apart?
options: The weeping mingled with the great shout of joy

4.title: Zerubbabel's refusal
4.text: Zerubbabel and Jeshua refuse the adversaries' help, saying they alone would build the house to the Lord, as Cyrus had commanded.

4.question: What did the people of the land do to the people of Judah?
options: Weakened their hands and troubled them in building

4.question: Who had transported the peoples to dwell in Samaria?
What did the letter ask the king to find in the book of records?
options: That Jerusalem was a rebellious city, hurtful to kings

4.question: What did the king's search reveal about Jerusalem's past?
options: Mighty kings had ruled over all beyond the river
There had never been kings there

4.question: Until when did the work of the house of God cease?
options: Until the second year of the reign of Darius
Until the seventh year of Artaxerxes

5.title: The prophets who stirred the work
5.text: The prophets Haggai and Zechariah, son of Iddo, prophesied in the name of the God of Israel to the Jews in Judah and Jerusalem, encouraging the rebuilding.

5.question: Why were the Jewish elders not stopped?
options: The eye of their God was upon them

5.question: What did Tatnai ask the elders?
options: Who commanded them to build the house

5.question: Who gave the original order to build the house of God?
What did the letter ask Darius to do?
options: Search the treasure house whether Cyrus's decree existed

6.title: The decree found
6.text: In the scroll found at Achmetha was Cyrus's decree commanding the house of God to be rebuilt, with the cost paid from the king's house.

6.question: What did the decree command to be restored to the temple in Jerusalem?
options: The gold and silver vessels taken by Nebuchadnezzar

6.question: For whom were the priests to pray when offering sacrifices?
options: For the life of the king and his sons

6.question: When was the temple finished?
options: In the sixth year of the reign of Darius
In the first year of Cyrus
In the seventh year of Artaxerxes
In the second year of Darius

6.question: Which feast did they keep seven days with joy?

7.title: Ezra the scribe
7.text: Ezra was a scribe skilled in the law of Moses, and the king granted him all he asked, because the hand of the Lord his God was upon him.

7.question: From where did Ezra go up toward Jerusalem?
When did Ezra arrive at Jerusalem?
options: On the first day of the fifth month
On the first day of the first month

7.question: What was Ezra to carry to the God of Israel in Jerusalem?
options: The silver and gold given by the king and his counsellors

7.question: From where would the rest of what was needed for the house of God come?
What was Ezra to do regarding those who knew the laws of God?
options: Set magistrates and judges to judge the people

8.title: The fast by the river Ahava
8.text: Ezra camped by the river Ahava and, seeing there were no Levites, sent for them; there he proclaimed a fast to seek of God a safe way.

8.question: How many men came with Elioenai, of the sons of Pahath-moab?
Who was the chief of the sons of Shephatiah who went up with Ezra?
How many men came with the last sons of Adonikam?
Why did Ezra send messengers to Iddo at Casiphia?
options: To bring ministers for the house of God

8.question: Why was Ezra ashamed to ask the king for an army?
options: He had said God's hand protects those who seek him
There was no danger on the way

8.question: What else did Ezra weigh besides the silver and gold?
options: Twenty gold basins and two vessels of fine bronze

8.question: How long did the group rest on arriving at Jerusalem?
What did the satraps and governors do on receiving the king's orders?
options: They helped the people and the house of God

9.title: Ezra's mourning
9.text: On hearing of the mingling with the pagan peoples, Ezra tore his garment and mantle, plucked the hair from his head and beard, and sat down astonished.

9.question: What did Ezra confess in prayer?
options: That their iniquities had grown up to the heavens
That the temple was not built

9.question: Through whom had God commanded not to mingle with the peoples of the land?
How does Ezra describe the remaining people before God?
options: As those who escaped, yet stand in their trespass

10.title: Shecaniah's proposal
10.text: Shecaniah acknowledges the sin of marrying foreign women, but declares that 'there is hope in Israel concerning this thing' and proposes a covenant with God.

10.question: What did Ezra make the chief men and all Israel do?
options: Swear that they would do according to the word

10.question: What did Ezra the priest declare to the people?
options: That they had transgressed by marrying foreign women
That they must return to Babylon
That the temple would be destroyed

10.question: Why did the people ask that the leaders handle the matter gradually?
options: The people were many and it was a time of heavy rain

10.question: What did the guilty offer for their trespass?
Who is mentioned among the singers in the list?
Whose children are listed in verse 29?
Whose children are listed in verse 33?
What is said of all those listed at the chapter's end?
